title: Wireless Zone Security Monitoring — Unknown Clients on OT SSID

spl:
index=wireless sourcetype="meraki:association" OR sourcetype="cisco:wlc" ssid="OT-PROD-*" earliest=-24h
| iplocation src_ip
| lookup ot_wireless_allowlist.csv client_mac OUTPUT asset_class
| where asset_class!="instrumentation"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by client_mac, ssid, ap_name
| sort - last_seen
description: Lists Wi-Fi associations to OT WLANs from unknown MAC vendors or non-instrumentation OUI prefixes.
value: Lists Wi-Fi associations to OT WLANs from unknown MAC vendors or non-instrumentation OUI prefixes.
implementation: (1) WPA3-Enterprise with cert; (2) dynamic PSK per device class; (3) block consumer OUIs; (4) RF site survey after changes.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Sessions.All_Sessions by All_Sessions.action, All_Sessions.src, All_Sessions.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: Lists Wi-Fi associations to OT WLANs from unknown MAC vendors or non-instrumentation OUI prefixes — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: WLAN controller syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `meraki:airmarshal` or `cisco:wlc`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) WPA3-Enterprise with cert; (2) dynamic PSK per device class; (3) block consumer OUIs; (4) RF site survey after changes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=wireless sourcetype="meraki:association" OR sourcetype="cisco:wlc" ssid="OT-PROD-*" earliest=-24h
| iplocation src_ip
| lookup ot_wireless_allowlist.csv client_mac OUTPUT asset_class
| where asset_class!="instrumentation"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by client_mac, ssid, ap_name
| sort - last_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**Wireless Zone Security Monitoring — Unknown Clients on OT SSID (IEC 62443-3-2 / Wireless zone security monitoring)** — Lists Wi-Fi associations to OT WLANs from unknown MAC vendors or non-instrumentation OUI prefixes.

Documented **Data sources**: `meraki:airmarshal` or `cisco:wlc`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): WLAN controller syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: wireless; **sourcetype**: meraki:association, cisco:wlc. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=wireless, sourcetype="meraki:association", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Pipeline stage (see **Wireless Zone Security Monitoring — Unknown Clients on OT SSID (IEC 62443-3-2 / Wireless zone security monitoring)**): iplocation src_ip
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where asset_class!="instrumentation"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by client_mac, ssid, ap_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Sessions.All_Sessions by All_Sessions.action, All_Sessions.src, All_Sessions.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Wireless Zone Security Monitoring — Unknown Clients on OT SSID (IEC 62443-3-2 / Wireless zone security monitoring)** — Lists Wi-Fi associations to OT WLANs from unknown MAC vendors or non-instrumentation OUI prefixes.

Documented **Data sources**: `meraki:airmarshal` or `cisco:wlc`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): WLAN controller syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Sessions.All_Sessions` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Map, Time chart.
